challenges.title: Base and Derived Classes
challenges.description:
Write a C++ program that defines a base class `Animal` with a public member variable `name` (string) and a method `speak()` that prints a generic message. Then define a derived class `Dog` that publicly inherits from `Animal`. Assign your C++ code to the variable `cpp`.

Your program must:
- Define a class Animal with a string member and a speak() method
- Define a class Dog that inherits from Animal using public inheritance
- Include a main() function that creates a Dog object
challenges.test_code:
assert 'class Animal' in cpp, 'Should define a base class named Animal'
assert 'class Dog' in cpp, 'Should define a derived class named Dog'
assert ': public Animal' in cpp or ':public Animal' in cpp, 'Dog should use public inheritance from Animal'
assert 'speak' in cpp, 'Should have a speak() method'
assert 'main()' in cpp or 'main ()' in cpp, 'Should have a main function'
assert 'return 0' in cpp, 'main should return 0'
hints: Define the base class first: class Animal { public: void speak() { ... } };
Use a colon for inheritance: class Dog : public Animal { };

challenges.title: Virtual Functions and Polymorphism
challenges.description:
Write a C++ program that demonstrates polymorphism using virtual functions. Define a base class `Shape` with a virtual method `area()` that returns a double. Define a derived class `Rectangle` that overrides `area()` to compute width * height. Use a base class pointer to call the overridden method. Assign your C++ code to the variable `cpp`.

Your program must:
- Define Shape with a virtual area() method
- Define Rectangle that overrides area()
- Use a Shape pointer to a Rectangle object and call area()
challenges.test_code:
assert 'class Shape' in cpp, 'Should define a base class Shape'
assert 'class Rectangle' in cpp, 'Should define a derived class Rectangle'
assert 'virtual' in cpp, 'Should use virtual keyword for polymorphism'
assert 'area' in cpp, 'Should have an area() method'
assert 'override' in cpp or '// override' in cpp or 'area()' in cpp, 'Derived class should override the area method'
assert 'Shape*' in cpp or 'Shape *' in cpp, 'Should use a base class pointer for polymorphism'
assert 'main()' in cpp or 'main ()' in cpp, 'Should have a main function'
assert 'return 0' in cpp, 'main should return 0'
hints: Declare area() as virtual in Shape: virtual double area() { return 0; }
Use a pointer: Shape* s = new Rectangle(5, 3); cout << s->area();
